User task:

Theorem title: Itô's formula for a twice continuously differentiable function of Brownian motion

Ambient theorem statement:
Itô's formula for a twice continuously differentiable function of Brownian motion.

Let \((B_t)_{t\ge 0}\) be a standard one-dimensional Brownian motion, let \(T>0\), and let \(f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) be a \(C^2\) function. Assume for simplicity that \(f'\) and \(f''\) are bounded and that \(f''\) is uniformly continuous on \(\mathbb{R}\). Then for every \(t\in[0,T]\),
\[
f(B_t)
=
f(B_0)
+
\int_0^t f'(B_s)\,dB_s
+
\frac12\int_0^t f''(B_s)\,ds.
\]


Target node:

{
  "id": "deterministic_taylor_expansion",
  "title": "Deterministic Taylor expansion for increments",
  "informal_statement": "Let $f:\\mathbb{R}\\to\\mathbb{R}$ be a $C^2$ function and let $\\omega$ be a modulus of continuity for $f''$. For any finite sequence of real numbers $B_{t_0}, B_{t_1}, \\dots, B_{t_n}$, let $\\Delta B_k = B_{t_{k+1}}-B_{t_k}$. Then\n\\[\nf(B_{t_n})-f(B_{t_0}) = \\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} f'(B_{t_k})\\,\\Delta B_k + \\frac12 \\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} f''(B_{t_k})(\\Delta B_k)^2 + R_n,\n\\]\nwhere the remainder $R_n$ satisfies\n\\[\n|R_n| \\le \\frac12\\,\\omega\\!\\left(\\max_{0 \\le k \\le n-1} |\\Delta B_k|\\right)\\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}(\\Delta B_k)^2.\n\\]",
  "informal_proof_text": "We begin with the telescoping identity $f(B_{t_n})-f(B_{t_0}) = \\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}\\bigl(f(B_{t_{k+1}})-f(B_{t_k})\\bigr)$.\nApplying Taylor's theorem with remainder to each increment, there exists $\\xi_k$ between $B_{t_k}$ and $B_{t_{k+1}}$ such that\n\\[\nf(B_{t_{k+1}}) = f(B_{t_k}) + f'(B_{t_k})\\,\\Delta B_k + \\frac12 f''(B_{t_k}) (\\Delta B_k)^2 + \\frac12\\bigl(f''(\\xi_k)-f''(B_{t_k})\\bigr)(\\Delta B_k)^2.\n\\]\nSumming over $k$, we obtain the stated identity with remainder\n\\[\nR_n = \\frac12\\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}\\bigl(f''(\\xi_k)-f''(B_{t_k})\\bigr)(\\Delta B_k)^2.\n\\]\nSince $\\xi_k$ lies between $B_{t_k}$ and $B_{t_{k+1}}$, we have $|\\xi_k - B_{t_k}| \\le |\\Delta B_k|$. By the definition of the modulus of continuity, $|f''(\\xi_k)-f''(B_{t_k})| \\le \\omega(|\\Delta B_k|) \\le \\omega(\\max_j |\\Delta B_j|)$. The bound on $|R_n|$ then follows from the triangle inequality.",
  "formalization_priority": 1,
  "formalization_rationale": "An entirely deterministic, algebraic inequality that isolates the analytic Taylor expansion step from the stochastic elements. It has extremely clear boundaries and is straightforward to formalize, making it a perfect, high-yield formal island."
}

Theorem title: Itô's formula for a twice continuously differentiable function of Brownian motion

Ambient theorem statement:
Itô's formula for a twice continuously differentiable function of Brownian motion.

Let \((B_t)_{t\ge 0}\) be a standard one-dimensional Brownian motion, let \(T>0\), and let \(f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) be a \(C^2\) function. Assume for simplicity that \(f'\) and \(f''\) are bounded and that \(f''\) is uniformly continuous on \(\mathbb{R}\). Then for every \(t\in[0,T]\),
\[
f(B_t)
=
f(B_0)
+
\int_0^t f'(B_s)\,dB_s
+
\frac12\int_0^t f''(B_s)\,ds.
\]


Target node:

{
  "id": "deterministic_taylor_expansion",
  "title": "Deterministic Taylor expansion for increments",
  "informal_statement": "Let $f:\\mathbb{R}\\to\\mathbb{R}$ be a $C^2$ function and let $\\omega$ be a modulus of continuity for $f''$. For any finite sequence of real numbers $B_{t_0}, B_{t_1}, \\dots, B_{t_n}$, let $\\Delta B_k = B_{t_{k+1}}-B_{t_k}$. Then\n\\[\nf(B_{t_n})-f(B_{t_0}) = \\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} f'(B_{t_k})\\,\\Delta B_k + \\frac12 \\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} f''(B_{t_k})(\\Delta B_k)^2 + R_n,\n\\]\nwhere the remainder $R_n$ satisfies\n\\[\n|R_n| \\le \\frac12\\,\\omega\\!\\left(\\max_{0 \\le k \\le n-1} |\\Delta B_k|\\right)\\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}(\\Delta B_k)^2.\n\\]",
  "informal_proof_text": "We begin with the telescoping identity $f(B_{t_n})-f(B_{t_0}) = \\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}\\bigl(f(B_{t_{k+1}})-f(B_{t_k})\\bigr)$.\nApplying Taylor's theorem with remainder to each increment, there exists $\\xi_k$ between $B_{t_k}$ and $B_{t_{k+1}}$ such that\n\\[\nf(B_{t_{k+1}}) = f(B_{t_k}) + f'(B_{t_k})\\,\\Delta B_k + \\frac12 f''(B_{t_k}) (\\Delta B_k)^2 + \\frac12\\bigl(f''(\\xi_k)-f''(B_{t_k})\\bigr)(\\Delta B_k)^2.\n\\]\nSumming over $k$, we obtain the stated identity with remainder\n\\[\nR_n = \\frac12\\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}\\bigl(f''(\\xi_k)-f''(B_{t_k})\\bigr)(\\Delta B_k)^2.\n\\]\nSince $\\xi_k$ lies between $B_{t_k}$ and $B_{t_{k+1}}$, we have $|\\xi_k - B_{t_k}| \\le |\\Delta B_k|$. By the definition of the modulus of continuity, $|f''(\\xi_k)-f''(B_{t_k})| \\le \\omega(|\\Delta B_k|) \\le \\omega(\\max_j |\\Delta B_j|)$. The bound on $|R_n|$ then follows from the triangle inequality.",

}

import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.ContDiff.Basic

open Set

lemma one_step_taylor (f : ℝ → ℝ) (hf : ContDiff ℝ 2 f) (x x₀ : ℝ) :
